results.name: TA17-164A: HIDDEN COBRA – North Korea’s DDoS Botnet Infrastructure
results.description: Internet Protocol (IP) addresses associated with a malware variant, known as DeltaCharlie, used to manage North Korea’s distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) botnet infrastructure. This alert contains indicators of compromise (IOCs), malware descriptions, network signatures, and host-based rules to help network defenders detect activity conducted by the North Korean government. The U.S. Government refers to the malicious cyber activity by the North Korean government as HIDDEN COBRA.

results.name: TA17-164A - HIDDEN COBRA DDoS Botnet Infrastructure
results.description: This joint Technical Alert (TA) is the result of analytic efforts between the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) and the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI). This alert provides technical details on the tools and infrastructure used by cyber actors of the North Korean government to target the media, aerospace, financial, and critical infrastructure sectors in the United States and globally. Working with U.S. Government partners, DHS and FBI identified Internet Protocol (IP) addresses associated with a malware variant, known as DeltaCharlie, used to manage North Korea’s distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) botnet infrastructure. This alert contains indicators of compromise (IOCs), malware descriptions, network signatures, and host-based rules to help network defenders detect activity conducted by the North Korean government. The U.S. Government refers to the malicious cyber activity by the North Korean government as HIDDEN COBRA.

results.name: Alert (TA17-164A)
results.description: HIDDEN COBRA – North Korean Malicious Cyber Activity

results.name: Alert (TA17-164A) HIDDEN COBRA – North Korea’s DDoS Botnet Infrastructure
results.description: US-CERT published about "HIDDEN COBRA". Official STIX/CSV IOC file is existing in web site of US-CERT. Please see Adversary below.

results.name: HIDDEN COBRA – North Korea’s DDoS Botnet Infrastructure | US-CERT
HIDDEN COBRA – North Korea’s DDoS Botnet Infrastructure
results.description:
Overview
This joint Technical Alert (TA) is the result of analytic efforts between the Department of
Homeland Security (DHS) and the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI). This alert provides
technical details on the tools and infrastructure used by cyber actors of the North Korean
government to target the media, aerospace, financial, and critical infrastructure sectors in the
United States and globally. Working with U.S. Government partners, DHS and FBI identified
Internet Protocol (IP) addresses associated with a malware variant, known as DeltaCharlie,
used to manage North Korea’s distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) botnet infrastructure. This
alert contains indicators of compromise (IOCs), malware descriptions, network signatures,
and host-based rules to help network defenders detect activity conducted by the North Korean
government. The U.S. Government refers to the malicious cyber activity by the North Korean
government as HIDDEN COBRA.
results.adversary: North Korean cyber Operations [Destover, Wild Positron/Duuzer, and Hangman]
